symbols: triple tiara (triregnum) — papal authority over the Church militant, suffering, and triumphant
crosier (pastoral staff) — the pope's role as universal shepherd of the Christian flock
gold cope — liturgical splendor and the majesty of the papal office
jeweled clasp (morse) — the precious nature of the faith entrusted to the papacy
white beard — age, wisdom, and the weight of ecclesiastical responsibility
dark background — solemnity and gravitas of the office
description: A half-length portrait of an elderly man with a long white beard wearing the papal triple tiara adorned with gold crowns and jewels. He is draped in a rich gold cope fastened at the chest with an ornate jeweled clasp. In his left hand he holds a crosier. His expression is stern and resolute, conveying the gravity of his office. The background is dark and unadorned, focusing all attention on the papal figure.
